message:
fix(tests): make 2 fastmcp-server tests Windows-correct

After 72462ff brought 8 of 9 jobs green, two genuine Windows-specific
test bugs surfaced (they had been masked by the earlier dead-code
crash, which never let the suite get this far).

1. **`providers::filesystem::test_validate_path_rejects_absolute_and_parent_escape`** —
   the test fed `/tmp/absolute.txt` to `FilesystemProvider::validate_path`
   expecting a `PathTraversal` rejection.  On Windows that string is a
   *relative* path (no drive letter), so `Path::is_absolute()` returns
   false, the rejection branch never fires, and the assert misfires.
   Pick a platform-appropriate absolute path with `cfg!(windows)` —
   `C:\Windows\System32\absolute.txt` on Windows, the original
   `/tmp/absolute.txt` everywhere else.  Both inputs are absolute on
   their respective platforms and exercise the same rejection branch.

2. **`lib_unit_tests::http_returning_server_honors_cancellation_without_needing_accept_wakeup`** —
   the test asserts that calling `cx.cancel_with(...)` makes
   `run_http_returning_with_cx` return promptly *without* a follow-up
   TCP connect to wake `accept()`.  That guarantee is a Unix
   accept()/epoll behavior; on Windows the IOCP-backed listener does
   not unblock from a non-I/O signal, so the documented fallback path
   (a connect to the listening port) is required there.  As long as
   the server *does* shut down within the fallback window — which
   `server_thread.join()` already verifies above the assertion —
   the cancellation contract is honored on Windows too.  Gate the
   strict no-wakeup-needed assertion behind `#[cfg(not(windows))]`
   and keep `returned_before_wakeup` consumed on Windows so rustc
   doesn't warn about the unused binding.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
